Keane-Collopy.description: Criminal organisation based in Limerick City, Ireland, founded in the 1990s by Christy Keane. Major drug suppliers in the Mid-West region, heavily involved in the Limerick feud since 2000. The gang has been weakened in recent years with key figures dead or imprisoned.

descriptions: The Hutch gang is allied with the Keane-Collopy Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Keane-Collopy Gang , McCarthy-Dundon Gang , RIRA , New IRA

descriptions: Keane-Collopy is allied with the Hutch Organised Crime Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Hutch Organised Crime Gang

descriptions: Keane-Collopy considers McCarthy-Dundon as rivals in the Limerick feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: McCarthy-Dundon Gang

descriptions: Keane-Collopy is in a violent feud with the Ryan Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The feud, which mainly involves the Keane-Collopys, the Ryans and the McCarthy-Dundon gang, has resulted in hundreds of violent incidents including stabbings, shootings and bomb attacks and the deaths of up to twenty people.

descriptions: The McCarthy-Dundon Gang and the Keane-Collopy Gang are both participants in the Limerick feud, which is a conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The feud, which involves several criminal families, mainly the McCarthy-Dundons, the Ryans and the Keane-Collopy gang from St. Mary's Park , has been ongoing since 2000.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Keane-Collopy Gang as a rival of the McCarthy-Dundon Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Keane-Collopy Gang

descriptions: Both were major rebel forces in the post-independence period, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: The immediate post-independence period saw the rise of the Communist Party of Burma (CPB) and Karen National Union (KNU) in particular as major rebel forces.

descriptions: MNDAA succeeded CPB control in Kokang area after CPB disbanded.
edges.evidence_quote: From the 1960s to 1989, the Kokang area ... was controlled by the Communist Party of Burma, and after the party's armed wing disbanded in 1989 it became a special region ... under the control of the Myanmar Nationalities Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA)

descriptions: NDAA is a splinter group that broke away from CPB.
edges.evidence_quote: A splinter group led by Sai Leun which broke away from the CPB at the same time established the National Democratic Alliance Army (NDAA), which controls the Mong La area (Special Region 4) of Shan State.

descriptions: People's Liberation Army is the armed wing of the Communist Party of Burma.
edges.evidence_quote: The Communist Party of Burma rearmed itself and announced the creation of its new armed wing, the People's Liberation Army, in late 2021.

descriptions: UWSA split from the CPB after internal mutiny.
edges.evidence_quote: Following the CPB's internal mutiny on 16 April 1989, ethnic Wa cadres ... announced their split from the party and the founding of the United Wa State Party and United Wa State Army

descriptions: SSA and CPB were in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In northern Shan State, the SSA came into conflict with the Communist Party of Burma (CPB), which had long been active along Burma's border with China.

descriptions: UWSA formed after CPB's armed wing collapsed.
edges.evidence_quote: The UWSA was formed after the collapse of the armed wing of the Communist Party of Burma (CPB) in 1989.

'Ndrina Bruzzaniti.description: The 'Ndrina Bruzzaniti is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, a Mafia-type criminal organization based in Calabria, Italy. It originates from Africo and is also active in the Milan area. The clan is allied with the Morabito, Palamara, and Crisafulli 'ndrine. Its history spans from the 1970s to the present, with recent events noted in the 2020s.

descriptions: The Bruzzaniti are a 'ndrina (sub-group) of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: I Bruzzaniti sono una 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta originaria di Africo.

descriptions: The Bruzzaniti are allied with the Crisafulli.
edges.evidence_quote: alleati dei Morabito , dei Palamara e dei Crisafulli .

descriptions: The Bruzzaniti are allied with the Morabito.
edges.evidence_quote: alleati dei Morabito , dei Palamara e dei Crisafulli .

descriptions: The Bruzzaniti are allied with the Palamara.
edges.evidence_quote: alleati dei Morabito , dei Palamara e dei Crisafulli .

descriptions: The Bruzzaniti (as part of Morabito-Bruzzaniti-Palamara) trafficked drugs with the Casalesi Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Inoltre trafficavano in droga con la cosca camorrista dei casalesi .

descriptions: Mazzaferro has contacts with Bruzzaniti.
edges.evidence_quote: ma hanno contatti anche con i Barbaro , i Calabrò, i Bruzzaniti , i Morabito e i Raso-Albanese

descriptions: Morabito is allied with Bruzzaniti in Milan.
edges.evidence_quote: In Milan, the Morabito 'ndrina main allies are the Bruzzaniti and Palamara 'ndrine

descriptions: The Morabito and Bruzzaniti are mentioned together as targets of an anti-mafia operation, indicating they are part of the same criminal network.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 3 maggio 2007 è stata effettuata una vasta operazione antimafia a Milano e in altre città d' Italia , contro i Morabito , Bruzzaniti e Palamara

descriptions: Bruzzaniti are main allies of Morabito in Milan.
edges.evidence_quote: A Milano i principali alleati dei Morabito sono i Bruzzaniti e i Palamara

descriptions: The Palamara and Bruzzaniti are mentioned together as targets of an anti-mafia operation, indicating they are part of the same criminal network.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 3 maggio 2007 è stata effettuata una vasta operazione antimafia a Milano e in altre città d' Italia , contro i Morabito , Bruzzaniti e Palamara

descriptions: Palamara is allied with Bruzzaniti.
edges.evidence_quote: I Palamara o Palamara-Staiti sono una 'ndrina di Africo alleata dei Bruzzaniti e dei Morabito .

descriptions: The Corleonesi were rivals of Gaetano Badalamenti's faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gaetano Badalamenti

descriptions: Galloping Goose expanded into territory previously controlled by Pharaohs, indicating a conflict over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: An expert on outlaw motorcycle gangs from Missouri State Highway Patrol said the Galloping Goose were expanding into territory formerly controlled by the Pharaohs motorcycle club during the 1980s and 1990s.

descriptions: The Crazy Dragons succeeded the Trang gang after it folded.
edges.evidence_quote: The Crazy Dragons were the successor gang to the Vietnamese Trang gang, which folded after a series of convictions of its members in 2002.

New Zealand Nomads.description: The New Zealand Nomads are a large organised criminal gang formed in Horowhenua, New Zealand in 1977 by Dennis Hines. Originally part of the Black Power gang, they split to form their own gang. They are involved in drug dealing, theft, murder, and extortion, and have a long-standing rivalry with the Mongrel Mob. The gang remains active with members in prison as of 2013.

descriptions: New Zealand Nomads are listed as allies of Black Power in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Crips , Killa Beez (gang) , King Cobras (gang) New Zealand Nomads

descriptions: Highway 61 members murdered a member of the New Zealand Nomads, leading to convictions in 1997.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997, Highway 61 members were convicted of murdering a member of the New Zealand Nomads.

descriptions: The Nomads split from Black Power, indicating a structural tie as a former faction.
edges.evidence_quote: The Nomads were originally members of the Black Power, known as the "Black Power Nomads", before a large portion of the gang split and formed their own gang in 1977.

descriptions: The infobox lists Head Hunters as an ally of the Nomads.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black Power, Head Hunters

descriptions: Tensions between the Nomads and Highway 61 led to the murder of a Nomad member.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997, tensions heated up with the Highway 61 gang and resulted in the murder of Nomad Malcom Munns.

descriptions: The infobox lists Mongrel Mob as a rival of the Nomads.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mongrel Mob

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bad Troublesome Ward, New Zealand Nomads , Black Power

descriptions: Individuals acted as referents for both Clan Cava and Clan Ruocco-Somma, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 25 marzo 2015 vengono arrestati Bruno Giugliano ... e Salvatore Notaro ... ritenuti i referenti nel Nolano dei clan "Cava" di Quindici e "Ruocco-Somma" di Piazzolla di Nola

descriptions: Both Chinese and Taiwanese syndicates operate fraud factories together.
edges.evidence_quote: Fraud factories are often operated by Chinese and Taiwanese criminal syndicates based in Southeast Asia.

descriptions: KSS members were part of a National Alliance youth group.
edges.evidence_quote: A second KSS chapter was founded in nearby Lancaster, which had a small group of white power skinheads who were part of a National Alliance youth group.

descriptions: The Order distributed robbery proceeds to the National Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Proceeds from these robberies were distributed to leaders of sympathetic organizations such as William Pierce (National Alliance) and Frazier Glenn Miller, Jr. (White Patriot Party).

descriptions: Both organizations use the same neo-Nazi symbol, but no direct relationship is asserted.
edges.evidence_quote: The logo of Volksfront was the Algiz rune, a common rune used as a neo-Nazi symbol common among other organizations such as National Alliance.

descriptions: Cohen raised funds for Irgun and provided security for their meetings.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1947 Ben Hecht of the Zionist American League for a Free Palestine approached Cohen asking for his help to raise funds for the Jewish paramilitary group Irgun. Cohen agreed and a fundraiser was held at his Slapsy Maxie's Café in LA, reportedly raising $200,000

descriptions: Bandidos absorbed Road Eagles chapters by patching them over.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos expanded into Germany in June 2000 when the club "patched over" seventeen chapters of the Ghostrider's, Road Eagles and Destroyers that had been serving as probationary chapters since the previous year.

Grupo Flechas.description: Armed wing of the Cabrera Sarabia brothers' La Mayiza faction of the Sinaloa Cartel, created in 2020 by Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada to counter the CJNG. Operates primarily in Zacatecas and Guanajuato, engaging in drug trafficking, homicide, and armed attacks. After Zambada's arrest in 2024, leadership passed to his son Ismael Zambada Sicairos. The group is known for its paramilitary training and use of heavy weaponry.

edges.source: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima
descriptions: The infobox lists Grupo Flechas as an ally of the CSRL.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Grupo Escorpión , Grupo Flechas

edges.source: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima
descriptions: Grupo Flechas is listed as an ally of the Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Grupo Escorpión , Grupo Flechas

edges.target: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima
descriptions: Infobox lists Santa Rosa de Lima Cartel as an ally of Grupo Flechas.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima

descriptions: Infobox lists Cartel del Noreste as an ally of Grupo Flechas.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel del Noreste

descriptions: Grupo Flechas's main mission is to stop the advance of CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: su misión principal consiste en frenar el avance del Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación

descriptions: Grupo Flechas is the armed wing of the La Mayiza faction.
edges.evidence_quote: El Grupo Flechas también conocido como Operativa MZ/MF , es un brazo armado de los hermanos Cabrera Sarabia de Durango de la facción La Mayiza del Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Grupo Flechas and Los Cascos engaged in intermittent clashes.
edges.evidence_quote: armed confrontations between Flechas aligned groups and Chapitos affiliated elements, often referred to as 'Los Cascos' — continued intermittently throughout the Sinaloa highlands

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Chapitos as an enemy of Grupo Flechas.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Los Chapitos

descriptions: Grupo Flechas supports MZ/MF faction in the conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: A Grupo Flechas convoy ... associated with the 'Los Cabrera' faction of MZ/MF was reportedly observed traveling from Durango toward Concordia, Sinaloa, to support MZ/MF forces

descriptions: Grupo Flechas provides weapons and funding to Cartel Nueva Plaza.
edges.evidence_quote: el Grupo Flechas le ha otorgado apoyo armamentistico y financiamiento a un grupo local denominado Cártel Nueva Plaza

descriptions: Grupo Flechas is an armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel's La Mayiza faction.
edges.evidence_quote: El Grupo Flechas también conocido como Operativa MZ/MF , es un brazo armado de los hermanos Cabrera Sarabia de Durango de la facción La Mayiza del Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: CJNG and Chapitos clashed with Grupo Flechas (MZ/MF).
edges.evidence_quote: On 17 December 2025, intense clashes were reported in Escuinapa, Sinaloa, between rival armed groups, likely involving Fuerzas Especiales Unión (FEU)—CJNG and Los Chapitos—against forces of "Grupo Flechas" of Mayito Flaco (MZ/MF).

descriptions: Infobox lists Grupo Flechas as an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Grupo Flechas

descriptions: CJNG considers Grupo Flechas an enemy per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Grupo Flechas

descriptions: The infobox lists Grupo Flechas as an enemy of CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Gobierno de México, Gobierno Federal de los Estados Unidos, Ejército Zapatista de Liberación Nacional, Grupos criminales : , Grupo Flechas, Cártel del Noreste, Cártel de Guasave, Cártel Nueva Plaza, Guerreros Unidos, Artistas Asesinos, Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima, La Familia Michoacana, Los Caballeros Templarios, Los Talibanes, Unión Tepito, Cártel de los Soles, Los Choneros

descriptions: Chapitos and CJNG clashed with Grupo Flechas (MZ/MF).
edges.evidence_quote: On 17 December 2025, intense clashes were reported in Escuinapa, Sinaloa, between rival armed groups, likely involving Fuerzas Especiales Unión (FEU)—CJNG and Los Chapitos—against forces of "Grupo Flechas" of Mayito Flaco (MZ/MF).

descriptions: Grupo Flechas is aligned with MF (MZ/MF).
edges.evidence_quote: On 8 January 2025, armed men alleged to belong to Grupo Flechas, aligned with MF, were observed entering El Palmito, Sinaloa.

descriptions: Grupo Flechas is a sub-group or ally of Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Grupo Flechas

United Wa State Army.description: The United Wa State Army is the military wing of the United Wa State Party, the de facto ruling party of Wa State in Myanmar. Formed in 1989 after the collapse of the Communist Party of Burma, it is a well-equipped ethnic minority army of 20,000–30,000 soldiers led by Bao Youxiang. It controls territory independent of Myanmar's central government but officially recognizes Myanmar's sovereignty. The UWSA is the largest non-state armed group in Myanmar and has led the Federal Political Negotiation and Consultative Committee since 2017.

descriptions: UWSA split from the CPB after internal mutiny.
edges.evidence_quote: Following the CPB's internal mutiny on 16 April 1989, ethnic Wa cadres ... announced their split from the party and the founding of the United Wa State Party and United Wa State Army

edges.source: Federal Political Negotiation and Consultative Committee
descriptions: United Wa State Army was a founding member of FPNCC.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 2017, the Northern Alliance, along with three other EAOs (the United Wa State Army, National Democratic Alliance Army, and the Shan State Army – North), formed the Federal Political Negotiation and Consultative Committee.

descriptions: Red Wa are associated with the United Wa State Army.
edges.evidence_quote: They are known for cooperating with the Red Wa (who are associated with the United Wa State Army) for the trafficking and sale of narcotics.

descriptions: United Wa State Army is listed as an ally of Sam Gor in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K, Bamboo Union, Big Circle Gang, Sun Yee On, Wo Shing Wo, Yamaguchi-gumi, United Wa State Army, Lebanese mafia, Comancheros, Hells Angels

descriptions: United Wa State Army provided military support to TNLA.
edges.evidence_quote: the TNLA received public support from ... other rebel groups like the KIA and United Wa State Army

descriptions: UWSA maintains close links with Arakan Army.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA maintains close links with FPNCC partners like ... the Arakan Army

descriptions: Arakan Army is listed as an ally of UWSA in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Non-state allies Arakan Army

descriptions: UWSA formed after CPB's armed wing collapsed.
edges.evidence_quote: The UWSA was formed after the collapse of the armed wing of the Communist Party of Burma (CPB) in 1989.

descriptions: Karenni National People's Liberation Front is an ally of UWSA.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Non-state allies ... Karenni National People's Liberation Front

descriptions: Karenni National People's Liberation Front is listed as an ally of UWSA in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Non-state allies ... Karenni National People's Liberation Front

descriptions: UWSA was in conflict with Mong Tai Army until 1996.
edges.evidence_quote: Until 1996 the United Wa State Army was involved in a conflict against the Shan Mong Tai Army led by drug kingpin Khun Sa

descriptions: Mong Tai Army is listed as an opponent of UWSA until 1996.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Non-state opponents Mong Tai Army (until 1996)

descriptions: UWSA maintains close links with MNDAA.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA maintains close links with FPNCC partners like the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA)

descriptions: NDAA is an ally of UWSA.
edges.evidence_quote: it serves as a link with its ally, the National Democratic Alliance Army (NDAA) in Mongla

descriptions: National Democratic Alliance Army is listed as an ally of UWSA in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Non-state allies ... National Democratic Alliance Army

descriptions: UWSA fights against Shan State Army (RCSS).
edges.evidence_quote: the Tatmadaw (Myanmar Armed Forces) has frequently allied with the UWSA to fight against Shan nationalist militia groups, such as the Shan State Army (RCSS)

descriptions: UWSA cut off weapons and financial aid to Shan State Army - North.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA declared that they would no longer provide weapons or financial aid to the TNLA, MNDAA, and Shan State Army - North

descriptions: Shan State National Army was an opponent of UWSA until 2005.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Non-state opponents ... Shan State National Army (until 2005)

descriptions: Shan State National Army is listed as an opponent of UWSA until 2005.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Non-state opponents ... Shan State National Army (until 2005)

descriptions: UWSA maintains close links with Ta'ang Army.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA maintains close links with FPNCC partners like ... the Ta'ang Army

descriptions: UWSA cut off weapons and financial aid to TNLA.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA declared that they would no longer provide weapons or financial aid to the TNLA, MNDAA, and Shan State Army - North

descriptions: Tatmadaw and UWSA have frequently allied against Shan groups.
edges.evidence_quote: the Tatmadaw (Myanmar Armed Forces) has frequently allied with the UWSA to fight against Shan nationalist militia groups

descriptions: Tatmadaw and UWSA have allied to fight Shan groups.
edges.evidence_quote: the Tatmadaw (Myanmar Armed Forces) has frequently allied with the UWSA to fight against Shan nationalist militia groups

descriptions: UWSA allegedly supplies weapons to Three Brotherhood Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA allegedly supplies weapons ... to anti-Junta groups such as the Three Brotherhood Alliance

descriptions: UWSA works with ULFA-ATF.
edges.evidence_quote: The United Wa State Army also works with the ULFA-ATF, an Assam resistance group

descriptions: United Liberation Front of Assam is listed as an ally of UWSA in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Non-state allies ... United Liberation Front of Assam

descriptions: UWSA is the armed wing of the United Wa State Party.
edges.evidence_quote: ethnic Wa cadres under the command of Bao Youxiang announced their split from the party and the founding of the United Wa State Party and United Wa State Army

descriptions: UWSA is the military wing of UWSP.
edges.evidence_quote: The United Wa State Army ... is the military wing of the United Wa State Party (UWSP)

descriptions: Wa National Army was an opponent of UWSA until 2007.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Non-state opponents ... Wa National Army (until 2007)

descriptions: Wa National Army is listed as an opponent of UWSA until 2007.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Non-state opponents ... Wa National Army (until 2007)

Stidda.description: The Stidda is a Sicilian Mafia-type criminal organization centered in southern Sicily, originating as a dissident offshoot of Cosa Nostra during the Second Mafia War in the early 1980s. It is active in rural areas and towns such as Agrigento, Caltanissetta, and Gela, engaging in racketeering, fraud, extortion, and murder. Unlike Cosa Nostra, it lacks an honor system and focuses solely on profit. Members often have a star tattoo, and the organization has been involved in violent conflicts with Cosa Nostra.

descriptions: The text lists Stidda among other mafias but does not assert a relationship with 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Cosa nostra , Camorra , Sacra Corona Unita e Stidda .

descriptions: The Milanese faction of Cursoti cooperated with the Stidda in arms and drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 17 ottobre 1993 l'inchiesta del GICO della Guardia di Finanza , coordinata dalla Direzione Distrettuale Antimafia di Firenze , portò all'arresto di Jimmy Miano ed altri appartenenti alla banda dei Cursoti milanesi, che avevano la loro base operativa presso l'autoparco di via Oreste Salomone a Milano , dove gestivano imponenti traffici di armi e droga insieme ad esponenti della Stidda di Gela

descriptions: Nuova Mala del Brenta colluded with Stidda members in fraudulent scams.
edges.evidence_quote: It is also known that Brenta mafiosi have colluded with Stidda members on a number of occasions involving a number of fraudulent scams at a Maltese casino in 2002.

descriptions: Stidda has a rivalry with Cosa Nostra but also exerts influence over it in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: nella parte centrale ed orientale dell'isola prevale invece una struttura di tipo orizzontale, non più verticistica (« ad assetto variabile », l'ha definita un rapporto della DIA), sul modello della Stidda e dei clan Cappello e Laudani, che ormai, dopo decenni di rivalità, esercitano un'enorme influenza su Cosa nostra, specialmente nel campo del traffico di stupefacenti

descriptions: Cosa Nostra fought against the Stidda in a violent conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Tra il 1986 e il 1991, Cosa nostra si trovò a fronteggiare una nuova organizzazione criminale, la cosiddetta "Stidda"

descriptions: Cosa Nostra and Stidda agreed to a truce and peaceful coexistence since 1991.
edges.evidence_quote: Cosa nostra rischiò di soccombere nello scontro e nel 1991 si trovò costretta a stipulare un patto di "non belligeranza" con gli stiddari ribelli, che prevedeva una ripartizione dei proventi illeciti in parti uguali tra le due organizzazioni, che da allora hanno iniziato una pacifica convivenza durata fino ad oggi.

descriptions: Infobox lists 'Ndrangheta as an ally of Stidda.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta, Camorra, Cosa Nostra

descriptions: Stidda follows the example of 'Ndrangheta, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Seguendo l'esempio di altre mafie tradizionali (come la 'ndrangheta )

descriptions: Infobox lists Camorra as an ally of Stidda.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta, Camorra, Cosa Nostra

descriptions: Stidda established relations with Camorra for drug supplies.
edges.evidence_quote: il clan gelese della Stidda ha stabilito importanti relazioni con il clan Cappello di Catania e con la camorra napoletana per imponenti forniture di cocaina , marijuana e hashish

descriptions: Stidda established relations with Clan Cappello for drug supplies.
edges.evidence_quote: il clan gelese della Stidda ha stabilito importanti relazioni con il clan Cappello di Catania e con la camorra napoletana per imponenti forniture di cocaina , marijuana e hashish

descriptions: The Stidda countered Cosa Nostra's power and expansion, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cosa Nostra wars of the late 1970s and early 1980s brought the Corleonesi clan and its vicious and ruthless leaders ... into power. This caused disorganization and disenchantment inside the traditional Cosa Nostra power base and values system, leaving the growing Stidda organization to counter Cosa Nostra's power, influence, and expansion in Southern and Eastern Sicily.

descriptions: Stidda is a rival and dissident offshoot of Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: It is most active in the rural parts of southern Sicily and is a rival to the Cosa Nostra, originating as a dissident offshoot group of the Sicilian Cosa Nostra.

descriptions: Infobox lists Cosa Nostra as a rival until the end of the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cosa Nostra (until the end of 1990s)

descriptions: Stidda originated from a conflict between a group that later formed Stidda and Cosa Nostra families.
edges.evidence_quote: alla fine degli anni '80 a Riesi ( CL ) si creò una dura contrapposizione tra la " famiglia " Cammarata, legata al boss latitante Giuseppe "Piddu" Madonia (rappresentante mafioso della provincia di Caltanissetta , fedele ai Corleonesi di Totò Riina ), e i mafiosi del gruppo Riggio-Annaloro-Stuppia

descriptions: Infobox lists Cosa Nostra as an ally of Stidda.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta, Camorra, Cosa Nostra

descriptions: Stidda and Cosa Nostra made peace and collaborated in criminal sectors.
edges.evidence_quote: a partire dalla primavera del 1991, arrivarono a stipulare una pace con gli stiddari Iannì-Cavallo per mettere fine agli scontri, che prevedeva una collaborazione tra le due organizzazioni nei principali settori criminali

descriptions: Stidda is described as a mafia-type organization, implicitly related to Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: La stidda è un' organizzazione criminale siciliana di tipo mafioso

descriptions: Black Gaza lists SouthPark Freestone Blood Gang as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cashion Park , SouthPark Freestone Blood Gang

descriptions: The Red Scorpions list Duhre group as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Duhre group (BC, Alberta, Ontario)

descriptions: Sahara Emirate Brigade is a group within AQIM until 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: Uqba ibn Nafi Brigade, Sahara Emirate Brigade (until 2017), Al-Mourabitoun Battalion (2015-2017)

descriptions: Salakau routinely attacked Sakongsa.
edges.evidence_quote: Attacks on rival gangs such as Sakongsa, Omega and 18 Sio Yi Ho were somewhat of a routine occurrence.

descriptions: Jund al-Aqsa is listed as an opponent since October 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Jund al-Aqsa (since October 2016)

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham opposed Jund al-Aqsa since October 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Jund al-Aqsa (since October 2016) [listed as opponent in infobox]

descriptions: Jund al-Aqsa is an opponent of Ahrar al-Sham since October 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Jund al-Aqsa (since October 2016)

descriptions: Al-Nusra Front was allied with Jund al-Aqsa.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Jund al-Aqsa

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Jund al-Aqsa.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Jund al-Aqsa

descriptions: Jund Al-Aqsa merged into Al-Nusra.
edges.evidence_quote: On 9 October, Jund Al-Aqsa, after infighting between them and numerous rebel groups including Ahrar Al-Sham, merged with JFS.

descriptions: AQKB was a former group under Al-Qaeda until 2010.
edges.evidence_quote: Former groups: ... AQKB , (until 2010)

descriptions: The text states the Bandidos are allied with the Mobshitters.
edges.evidence_quote: The Australian Bandidos are allied with the Diablos , Mobshitters and Rock Machine

descriptions: SGDs and Insane Two One Boys developed a friendship through incarceration.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 80s, members of the SGDs were incarcerated with members of the Insane Two One Boys, notably a Two One elder named Pepe, this brought a friendship with the two organizations that carried out to the streets.

descriptions: ANM controlled Heroes of Return.
edges.evidence_quote: the ANM established the Abtal al-Audah (Heroes of Return) commando group in early summer 1966. The ANM exercised de facto control, unbeknownst to the PLO, which funded the outfit.

descriptions: PLA collaborated with ANM to establish Heroes of Return.
edges.evidence_quote: In collaboration with the Palestine Liberation Organisation's (PLO) Palestinian Liberation Army (PLA), the ANM established the Abtal al-Audah (Heroes of Return) commando group in early summer 1966.
